children.heading: Limitation on use of grant for administrative purposes
children.label: b Limitation on use of grant for administrative purposes

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 1 Limitation
children.text: A State to which a grant is made under shall not expend more than 15 percent of the grant for administrative purposes.section 603 of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to the use of a grant for information technology and computerization needed for tracking or monitoring required by or under this part.

children.heading: Authority to treat interstate immigrants under rules of former State
children.label: c Authority to treat interstate immigrants under rules of former State
children.text: A State operating a program funded under this part may apply to a family the rules (including benefit amounts) of the program funded under this part of another State if the family has moved to the State from the other State and has resided in the State for less than 12 months.

children.heading: Authority to use portion of grant for other purposes
children.label: d Authority to use portion of grant for other purposes
A
children.text: Division A of subchapter XX of this chapter.

children.label: B
children.text: The Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 9857

children.heading: Limitation on amount transferable to division A of subchapter XX programs1See References in Text note below.1
children.label: 2 Limitation on amount transferable to division A of subchapter XX programs1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State may use not more than the applicable percent of the amount of any grant made to the State under for a fiscal year to carry out State programs pursuant to division A of subchapter XX.section 603(a) of this title1

children.heading: Applicable percent
children.label: B Applicable percent
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the applicable percent is 4.25 percent in the case of fiscal year 2001 and each succeeding fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B) of this paragraph, any amount paid to a State under this part that is used to carry out a State program pursuant to a provision of law specified in paragraph (1) shall not be subject to the requirements of this part, but shall be subject to the requirements that apply to Federal funds provided directly under the provision of law to carry out the program, and the expenditure of any amount so used shall not be considered to be an expenditure under this part.

children.heading: Exception relating to division A of subchapter XX programs1
children.label: B Exception relating to division A of subchapter XX programs1
children.text: All amounts paid to a State under this part that are used to carry out State programs pursuant to division A of subchapter XX shall be used only for programs and services to children or their families whose income is less than 200 percent of the income official poverty line (as defined by the Office of Management and Budget, and revised annually in accordance with ) applicable to a family of the size involved.1section 9902(2) of this title

children.heading: Authority to carry over certain amounts for benefits or services or for future contingencies
children.label: e Authority to carry over certain amounts for benefits or services or for future contingencies
children.text: A State or tribe may use a grant made to the State or tribe under this part for any fiscal year to provide, without fiscal year limitation, any benefit or service that may be provided under the State or tribal program funded under this part.

children.heading: Authority to operate employment placement program
children.label: f Authority to operate employment placement program
children.text: A State to which a grant is made under may use the grant to make payments (or provide job placement vouchers) to State-approved public and private job placement agencies that provide employment placement services to individuals who receive assistance under the State program funded under this part.section 603 of this title

children.heading: Implementation of electronic benefit transfer system
children.label: g Implementation of electronic benefit transfer system
children.text: A State to which a grant is made under is encouraged to implement an electronic benefit transfer system for providing assistance under the State program funded under this part, and may use the grant for such purpose.section 603 of this title

children.heading: Use of funds for individual development accounts
children.label: h Use of funds for individual development accounts

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A State to which a grant is made under may use the grant to carry out a program to fund individual development accounts (as defined in paragraph (2)) established by individuals eligible for assistance under the State program funded under this part.section 603 of this title

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: Under a State program carried out under paragraph (1), an individual development account may be established by or on behalf of an individual eligible for assistance under the State program operated under this part for the purpose of enabling the individual to accumulate funds for a qualified purpose described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Postsecondary educational expenses
children.label: i Postsecondary educational expenses
children.text: Postsecondary educational expenses paid from an individual development account directly to an eligible educational institution.

children.heading: First home purchase
children.label: ii First home purchase
children.text: Qualified acquisition costs with respect to a qualified principal residence for a qualified first-time homebuyer, if paid from an individual development account directly to the persons to whom the amounts are due.

children.heading: Business capitalization
children.label: iii Business capitalization
children.text: Amounts paid from an individual development account directly to a business capitalization account which is established in a federally insured financial institution and is restricted to use solely for qualified business capitalization expenses.

children.heading: Contributions to be from earned income
children.label: C Contributions to be from earned income
children.text: An individual may only contribute to an individual development account such amounts as are derived from earned income, as defined in section 911(d)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

children.heading: Withdrawal of funds
children.label: D Withdrawal of funds
children.text: The Secretary shall establish such regulations as may be necessary to ensure that funds held in an individual development account are not withdrawn except for 1 or more of the qualified purposes described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An individual development account established under this subsection shall be a trust created or organized in the United States and funded through periodic contributions by the establishing individual and matched by or through a qualified entity for a qualified purpose (as described in paragraph (2)(B)).

children.label: i
children.text: a not-for-profit organization described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from taxation under section 501(a) of such Code; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a State or local government agency acting in cooperation with an organization described in clause (i).

children.heading: No reduction in benefits
children.label: 4 No reduction in benefits
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of Federal law (other than the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) that requires consideration of 1 or more financial circumstances of an individual, for the purpose of determining eligibility to receive, or the amount of, any assistance or benefit authorized by such law to be provided to or for the benefit of such individual, funds (including interest accruing) in an individual development account under this subsection shall be disregarded for such purpose with respect to any period during which such individual maintains or makes contributions into such an account.

children.label: i
children.text: An institution described in section 1088(a)(1) or 1141(a) of title 20, as such sections are in effect on .August 22, 1996

children.label: ii
children.text: An area vocational education school (as defined in subparagraph (C) or (D) of ) which is in any State (as defined in ), as such sections are in effect on .section 2471(4) of title 20section 2471(33) of title 20August 22, 1996

children.label: i
children.text: tuition and fees required for the enrollment or attendance of a student at an eligible educational institution, and

children.label: ii
children.text: fees, books, supplies, and equipment required for courses of instruction at an eligible educational institution.

children.heading: Qualified acquisition costs
children.label: C Qualified acquisition costs
children.text: The term “qualified acquisition costs” means the costs of acquiring, constructing, or reconstructing a residence. The term includes any usual or reasonable settlement, financing, or other closing costs.

children.heading: Qualified business
children.label: D Qualified business
children.text: The term “qualified business” means any business that does not contravene any law or public policy (as determined by the Secretary).

children.heading: Qualified business capitalization expenses
children.label: E Qualified business capitalization expenses
children.text: The term “qualified business capitalization expenses” means qualified expenditures for the capitalization of a qualified business pursuant to a qualified plan.

children.heading: Qualified expenditures
children.label: F Qualified expenditures
children.text: The term “qualified expenditures” means expenditures included in a qualified plan, including capital, plant, equipment, working capital, and inventory expenses.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “qualified first-time homebuyer” means a taxpayer (and, if married, the taxpayer’s spouse) who has no present ownership interest in a principal residence during the 3-year period ending on the date of acquisition of the principal residence to which this subsection applies.

children.heading: Date of acquisition
children.label: ii Date of acquisition
children.text: The term “date of acquisition” means the date on which a binding contract to acquire, construct, or reconstruct the principal residence to which this subparagraph applies is entered into.

children.label: i
children.text: is approved by a financial institution, or by a nonprofit loan fund having demonstrated fiduciary integrity,

children.label: ii
children.text: includes a description of services or goods to be sold, a marketing plan, and projected financial statements, and

children.label: iii
children.text: may require the eligible individual to obtain the assistance of an experienced entrepreneurial advisor.

children.heading: Qualified principal residence
children.label: I Qualified principal residence
children.text: The term “qualified principal residence” means a principal residence (within the meaning of section 1034 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986), the qualified acquisition costs of which do not exceed 100 percent of the average area purchase price applicable to such residence (determined in accordance with paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 143(e) of such Code).

children.heading: Sanction welfare recipients for failing to ensure that minor dependent children attend school
children.label: i Sanction welfare recipients for failing to ensure that minor dependent children attend school
children.text: A State to which a grant is made under shall not be prohibited from sanctioning a family that includes an adult who has received assistance under any State program funded under this part attributable to funds provided by the Federal Government or under the supplemental nutrition assistance program, as defined in section 2012() of title 7, if such adult fails to ensure that the minor dependent children of such adult attend school as required by the law of the State in which the minor children reside.section 603 of this titlel1

children.heading: Requirement for high school diploma or equivalent
children.label: j Requirement for high school diploma or equivalent
children.text: A State to which a grant is made under shall not be prohibited from sanctioning a family that includes an adult who is older than age 20 and younger than age 51 and who has received assistance under any State program funded under this part attributable to funds provided by the Federal Government or under the supplemental nutrition assistance program, as defined in section 2012() of title 7, if such adult does not have, or is not working toward attaining, a secondary school diploma or its recognized equivalent unless such adult has been determined in the judgment of medical, psychiatric, or other appropriate professionals to lack the requisite capacity to complete successfully a course of study that would lead to a secondary school diploma or its recognized equivalent.section 603 of this titlel1

children.heading: Limitations on use of grant for matching under certain Federal transportation program
children.label: k Limitations on use of grant for matching under certain Federal transportation program
A
children.text: the grant is used for new or expanded transportation services (and not for construction) that benefit individuals described in subparagraph (C), and not to subsidize current operating costs;

children.label: B
children.text: the grant is used to supplement and not supplant other State expenditures on transportation;

children.label: i
children.text: recipients of assistance under the State program funded under this part;

children.label: ii
children.text: former recipients of such assistance;

children.label: iii
children.text: noncustodial parents who are described in ; andsection 603(a)(5)(C)(iii) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: low-income individuals who are at risk of qualifying for such assistance; and

children.label: D
children.text: the services provided through such use of the grant promote the ability of such recipients to engage in work activities (as defined in ).section 607(d) of this title

children.heading: Amount limitation
children.label: 2 Amount limitation
children.text: From a grant made to a State under , the amount that a State uses to match funds described in paragraph (1) of this subsection shall not exceed the amount (if any) by which 30 percent of the total amount of the grant exceeds the amount (if any) of the grant that is used by the State to carry out any State program described in subsection (d)(1) of this section.section 603(a) of this title

children.heading: Rule of interpretation
children.label: 3 Rule of interpretation
children.text: The provision by a State of a transportation benefit under a program conducted under section 3037 of the Transportation Equity Act for the 21st Century, to an individual who is not otherwise a recipient of assistance under the State program funded under this part, using funds from a grant made under , shall not be considered to be the provision of assistance to the individual under the State program funded under this part.section 603(a) of this title
